title: I-Team: Meeting between Mayor, Council President Pro Temp, Finance Chair on city budget
summary: Mayor Ben Walsh met with Council President Pro Temp Pat Hogan and Finance Committee Chair Corey Williams for 45 minutes to discuss the city budget amid a dispute over proposed amendments. The Common Council voted unanimously to reject Walsh's $348M budget proposal and cut $16M through 7% departmental reductions, based on independent analysis by Bonadio Group. Walsh indicated openness to compromise and has 10 days to issue vetoes on the 80 proposed amendments.

political_signal.description: Executive-legislative tension over FY2026 budget priorities. Council seeking $16M in cuts across all departments (7% reduction); Mayor signaling openness to compromise after unanimous Council rejection of original $348M proposal. Negotiation phase initiated.

processing_notes: Date metadata indicates May 2025 publication; verify current fiscal year context
Bonadio Group analysis cited as independent review source used by both Mayor and Council
Veto timeline: Mayor Walsh expected to announce vetoes on Monday following meeting
